name: Potato-Stuffed Enchiladas
recipeInstructions:
Sauce: Heat olive oil in a heavy skillet over medium heat. Add onions and garlic and sauté for 5 minutes until translucent. Mix in cumin seeds, chili powder, oregano, cinnamon, and sauté for 1 minute more. Stir in enchilada sauce and tomato sauce, simmering for 2 minutes. Add lime juice to taste with salt and cayenne pepper.
(Prepare the sauce up to 2 days in advance and reheat before use.)
Enchiladas: Heat olive oil in a large heavy skillet over medium heat. Add potatoes and cook until golden brown, stirring often, about 8 minutes. Reduce heat slightly. Stir in chili powder and cumin seeds, cooking for another minute. Add tomatoes, green onions, and tomato sauce. Cover and cook until the potatoes are tender, approximately 6 minutes. Mix in half of the jack cheese, egg, cilantro, and jalapeno. Season with salt and cayenne pepper.
Brush tortillas lightly with oil and briefly fry them in a hot nonstick pan on each side, about 30 seconds per side. Spoon one-third of the filling onto each tortilla and roll up securely, seam-side down, placing them in a baking dish. Preheat oven to 450°F (232°C). Pour sauce over the enchiladas, sprinkle with feta cheese, and remaining jack cheese on top. Cover the pan with foil and bake until heated through and bubbling, about 30 minutes. Serve with sour cream if desired.
